thesis: Acquisition odds remain above baseline because Reuters reported preliminary takeover interest (including potential Stripe interest), but no buyer has confirmed intent and reports still describe discussions as early and uncertain. In the absence of corroborated process milestones (board process, financing package, regulatory pre-clear signals), probability should stay conservative.
method: Started from a low base rate for mega-cap fintech takeouts and adjusted only for recent high-credibility reporting/filings, discounting unverified or preliminary rumor signals and requiring process evidence for larger moves.

evidence.signal: Reuters (citing Bloomberg) reported Stripe has preliminary interest in acquiring all or parts of PayPal; both firms declined comment and Reuters could not independently verify.
Reuters reported unsolicited buyer interest and bank meetings, while also stating interest was preliminary and might not lead to a transaction.
Reuters reported CEO change and weak 2026 guidance; analysts flagged possible review of strategic assets, which can raise receptivity to inbound bids.
PayPal's official Q4/FY2025 release framed execution as a standalone operating plan and provided no disclosed sale-process indicators.
